title: Aureline schema-evolution migration record row
description: Boundary schema for one schema_migration_record_row describing a single rename, move, split, merge, type change, default change, addition, removal, or alias step between versions of a human-editable artifact family. Rows are the diffable, reviewable unit that lets loaders, docs generators, migration tooling, compatibility-report surfaces, and support-export parity checks share one machine-readable migration history instead of relying on loader-only defaults or ad hoc per-surface rename tables. Companion artifacts live in docs/config/artifact_format_and_migration_policy.md (the normative policy), artifacts/config/format_selection_matrix.yaml (the artifact-family to format binding), and fixtures/config/comment_preservation_cases/ (worked examples). This schema governs per-artifact key and field evolution; it does NOT replace the stable-identifier lifecycle row frozen in schemas/governance/deprecation_metadata.schema.json nor the telemetry-family registry entry frozen in schemas/governance/schema_registry_entry.schema.json, both of which cover distinct concerns. Adding a new change_class, transform_kind, lossy_reason_class, unknown_field_posture, comment_preservation_posture, ordering_posture, disclosure_surface, or compatibility_window_unit value is additive-minor and requires a schema_migration_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

schema_migration_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the schema-migration-record vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence intended for reviewer surfaces, docs, manifests, migration guides, or audit rows.

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

stable_dot_id.description: Dot-separated stable identifier used for migration-record ids, artifact-family refs, and key paths on surfaces that use dotted keys.

owner_ref.description: Stable lower_snake identifier for the owning team, role, or governance body.

version_label.description: Release-line, artifact schema_version, or version label the migration applies between. Kept as free text because some artifact families key off release families, some off integer schema_version, and some off calendar labels.

schema_uri.description: Stable $id URI published by the JSON schema the artifact conforms to, when the artifact family is schema-backed. For YAML, JSONC, or TOML artifacts, this is the same URI the on-disk file carries in its $schema field or yaml-language-server header.

artifact_family_ref.description: Stable id of the artifact family this migration row governs. Resolves through artifacts/config/format_selection_matrix.yaml so the row's format, comment-preservation posture, and unknown-field posture are discoverable from the migration record alone.

change_class.description: Typed class of change this row represents. One row = one change. A rename_key that also narrows type is two rows, not one, so the diff review surface stays flat.

transform_kind.description: How the reader or writer computes the new value from the old one. identity_copy means the value carries over unchanged; drop_with_record_in_audit means the old value is intentionally discarded but an audit record must be emitted; not_applicable_additive_only is used when the change is purely additive and no transform is needed.

lossy_reason_class.description: Typed reason a migration is lossy. not_lossy is the only value that satisfies the gate when lossy_flag is false; any other value requires lossy_flag to be true and a named rationale.

unknown_field_posture.description: How the loader must treat fields it does not recognize on the post-migration shape. Anything other than preserve_verbatim on a human-edited artifact family requires an explicit policy row in artifacts/config/format_selection_matrix.yaml and a disclosure rule in docs/config/artifact_format_and_migration_policy.md.

comment_preservation_posture.description: How the migration treats comments attached to the old key or surrounding block. lossy_rewrite_disclosed is only valid when the row also records disclosure_surfaces that will carry the warning.

ordering_posture.description: How the migration treats block, array, and key order. canonical_reorder_disclosed is only valid when the row records disclosure_surfaces that will carry the reorder note.

compatibility_window_unit.description: Unit used in the structured compatibility window. Kept aligned with the interface-lifecycle support_window_unit vocabulary so tooling can reuse the same overlap-math helpers.

compatibility_window_rule.description: One overlap rule inside the compatibility window during which readers must still accept the old shape.

rule_mode.description: all_of = every listed window must hold before the old shape may be dropped. any_of = any one listed window is sufficient.

compatibility_window.description: Structured overlap window the old shape is honored for before the migration may drop it. Tied to the compatibility surface rather than to loader-only defaults.

disclosure_surface.description: Surface that must carry the migration or lossy-rewrite disclosure for the row. in_file_header_comment is the on-disk artifact header the writer emits when it rewrites a human-edited file.

rollback_posture.description: How an operator may roll back from the post-migration shape to the pre-migration shape. not_reversible_forward_only requires a rollback_note explaining why and what operators should do instead.

policy_doc_ref.description: Normative policy document the row cites. Defaults to docs/config/artifact_format_and_migration_policy.md but may point at a family-specific policy where one exists.

migration_guide_ref.description: Migration guide a consumer uses to migrate. Null is only acceptable for purely additive changes with transform_kind = not_applicable_additive_only.

evidence_refs.description: Worked fixtures, diff snapshots, or round-trip tests that demonstrate the migration behavior. Required for any lossy row.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixture rows.

migration_record_id.description: Stable dot-separated id for this migration-record row. Mint a new id rather than reusing an old one when a row is superseded.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the migration row.

description.description: One-sentence statement of what changed and why it is recorded as its own row.

schema_uri_ref.description: The $schema URI the post-migration artifact carries, or null when the artifact family is not schema-backed. Schema-backed families MUST populate this field.

version_field_ref.description: Name of the artifact's explicit version field (for example schema_version, document_version, or manifest_version). Null only when the artifact family's format-selection row records version_field_policy = not_applicable.

old_identifier.description: Old key path, field name, alias, or value being migrated from. Null only for change_class values that add a new identifier with no predecessor (add_optional_field, add_required_field, introduce_schema_uri, introduce_version_field).

new_identifier.description: New key path, field name, alias, or value being migrated to. Null only for change_class = remove_key_hard.

transform_note.description: Short prose statement of what the transform does in reader and writer terms. Required even when transform_kind = identity_copy so the audit trail reads cleanly.

lossy_flag.description: True when the migration cannot be reversed exactly. Any true value requires lossy_reason_class != not_lossy and a non-empty lossy_rationale.

lossy_rationale.description: Reviewable rationale for a lossy migration. Null only when lossy_flag is false and lossy_reason_class is not_lossy.

rollback_note.description: Short reviewable note describing what an operator must do to roll back, or why rollback is not available.

disclosure_surfaces.description: Surfaces that must carry the migration or lossy-rewrite disclosure.

notes.description: Optional additional note for future consumers.

allOf.description: Lossy rows must name a non-not_lossy reason and carry a rationale.
Hard removals have no successor identifier.
Purely additive rows have no predecessor identifier.
Non-removal rows must name a successor identifier.
Disclosed-rewrite comment posture requires a disclosure surface.
Canonical reorder posture requires a disclosure surface.
Non-additive and non-identity transforms require a migration guide link and at least one evidence ref.
Lossy rows must cite at least one worked fixture or evidence artifact.
Forward-only rollback posture requires a rollback note that explains why and what operators do instead.
